title: Oil jumps as U.S. fresh strikes on Iran raise worries of extended disruption to energy flows
summary: US military strikes on Iran drove oil prices higher Thursday amid concerns about potential energy supply disruptions. The geopolitical tensions could extend broader impacts on global energy markets.
content: Oil prices jumped on Thursday after the United States launched a fresh round of military strikes against targets in Iran.

zh.title: 美国新轮轰炸伊朗引发能源供应中断担忧，油价上涨
zh.summary: 美国军方对伊朗的轰炸周四推高油价，引发了对潜在能源供应中断的担忧。地缘政治紧张局势可能对全球能源市场产生更广泛的影响。

title: Oracle beats on earnings, but stock drops on plans to raise another $20 billion
summary: Oracle exceeded quarterly earnings expectations but announced plans to raise $20 billion for data center expansion, offsetting investor enthusiasm as negative free cash flow raised concerns. The capital needs reflect the high cost of AI infrastructure investment.
content: Oracle topped quarterly expectations, but free cash flow is negative, and the company plans to raise more capital for data center projects.

zh.title: 甲骨文盈利超预期，但因融资200亿美元计划股价下跌
zh.summary: 甲骨文超过了季度盈利预期，但宣布计划筹集200亿美元用于数据中心扩展，因负自由现金流引发的担忧抵消了投资者的热情。资本需求反映了AI基础设施投资的高成本。

title: My plumber charged $160 to fix the cistern on my toilet — but created another problem. Do I pay again?
summary: A homeowner questions whether they should pay additional charges after a plumber's repair work created a new problem with their toilet's constantly refilling cistern. The column addresses consumer rights when service work results in additional issues.
content: “The cistern on my toilet has been constantly refilling and making a hissing noise.”

zh.title: 水管工收费160美元修理马桶水箱，但制造了新问题。我应该再付钱吗？
zh.summary: 一位房主质疑在水管工的维修工作导致其马桶水箱持续加水的新问题后，他们是否应该支付额外费用。该专栏讨论了当服务工作导致额外问题时的消费者权益。
